title: nemophila heterophylla var. tenera
intro: Nemophila heterophylla var. tenera, also known as Baby Blue Eyes, is a flowering plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae) and is native to the southwestern United States. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to soothe and calm the digestive system, and it has been employed in various herbal preparations to treat ailments such as stomach issues and fever. The health benefits of Nemophila heterophylla var. tenera include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce swelling and promote wound healing. In herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
